title: camera
objectives: Preview and capture safely with the camera plugin

sections.markdown: The `camera` plugin (Flutter Team) provides low-level access to the platform camera: enumerate cameras, start a preview, capture stills, and record videos with a `CameraController`.

The camera is a scarce, expensive resource. Dispose the controller in `dispose()`; pause it in the app's `AppLifecycleState.paused` (background) and re-init on resume. Otherwise you'll leak sensors and drain battery.
Setting the highest `ResolutionPreset` for a UI preview → hot device + jank. Use `medium`/`high` for preview and switch just before capture, or use `imageFormatGroup` tuned to your ML/scan pipeline.

For scanning workflows (barcodes, ML Kit), prefer `mobile_scanner` or camera + ML Kit direct — the raw camera plugin gives you flexibility but more code. Watch orientation with WidgetsBindingObserver + didChangeMetrics.

flashcards.back: In State.dispose() AND on AppLifecycleState.paused; re-init on resume.
Overheats, drops frames, and burns battery. Use appropriate preset per stage.
mobile_scanner or camera + ML Kit direct, tuned for detection latency.

options: Dispose on route exit; pause on background
quiz.explain: Camera is expensive — free it when unused.

challenges.text: Show a camera preview + take a photo.
Add orientation lock and switch front/back cameras.
Handle lifecycle: pause/resume without leaking sensors.
Feed frames to ML Kit for realtime pose/text detection.

interviewQuestions: How do you manage the CameraController lifecycle?
Why does resolution matter for a preview?
How to keep power/temperature under control?
